name: PDF2Image

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# PDF2Image v1.3.1 - Release Notes
This release gives users full control over where and how their images are saved.

## Image Destination & Organization
**Custom Destination Folder** 
Images no longer have to land in the default destination folder. A new setting lets users turn off the default and pick a specific folder instead.

**Custom Folder Name** 
Previously, the output folder always took the name of the source PDF. A new setting lets users override this with a custom folder name.

**Per-Image Custom Naming**
Users can now name individual images via a new naming modal, which saves images under the chosen names rather than auto-generated ones.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# PDF2Image v1.3.0 – Release Notes
This major release introduces significant performance gains via multi-threaded processing, a completely reorganized Settings UI, and new control over image file formats. The core code has been heavily refactored for future stability and maintenance.

## Performance & Concurrency
The introduction of concurrent (multi-threaded) page processing drastically improves conversion speed for large PDF documents.
A more in-depth performance analysis can be found [here](https://github.com/RasmusAChr/PDF2Images/blob/4ae8f24f861a4c48c2efaf9ef27351d4df9b5d47/resources/PerformanceLog.pdf).
- **Massive Speedup**: Performance tests show speedups reaching over 3.1x and time savings of nearly 68% on large files.
    - Overall processing time for test files saw a speedup of approximately 2.54x (154% faster) on PC and 2.78x (178% faster) on the laptop.
- **Custom Concurrency Setting**: Added a new setting to allow users to configure the maximum number of pages to be processed concurrently.

## New Features & Settings
- **Configurable Image Formats**: Users can now select the output file type: WebP, PNG, or JPEG. WebP is the new default, offering smaller file sizes and faster loading.
- **New Settings Interface**: The settings page has been completely reorganized into distinct sections (Image Settings, Insertion Settings, Advanced Settings, etc.), making configuration more intuitive.
- **Header Extraction Out of Beta**: The feature to extract headers from PDF pages is now fully stable and out of beta.
- **Improved PDF Selection Modal**: The modal now correctly reflects all options, including a fix for the misspelled insertion method (changed from 'Procedual' to 'Procedural').

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# PDF2Image v1.2.9 – Release Notes

## Improvements
- **Preserve Cursor Position**: Improved the insertion process by preserving the initial cursor position when inserting a pdf. This allows users to move their cursor while inserting pdf files.
- Minor code enhancements.

## Bug Fixes
- **Header Pages**: Fixed an issue where pages containing only a header would not get the header extracted, due to the header sensitivity. Now header will be extracted on those pages.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# PDF2Image v1.2.8 – Release Notes

## New Features
- **Image Separator Options**: Added a flexible setting to insert separators between images, with options for no separation, empty line, separator line, or both. Includes additional control to add separators above the first image and below the last one.
- **Remove Duplicate Headers**: Introduced an option to automatically remove duplicate headers detected across consecutive PDF pages.
- **Image Quality Selector**: Users can now select image quality directly from the PDF import modal.

## Improvements
- **UI Enhancements**: Improved the PDF selector modal with a custom file selection button, clearer file name display, and a dropdown for image quality selection.
- **Settings Layout**: Rearranged plugin settings to improve clarity. Image separator settings now appear above the "Enable headers" option.

## Bug Fixes
- **File Name Handling**: Fixed an issue where PDFs with a `#` character in the filename could not be loaded.

# Version 1.2.7
## Bug fix
**Handling Blank Pages with only a header**
A bug was occurring where blank pages, displaying only the header, failed to generate the header text above the image.

## Other
Updated the readme file.

# Version 1.2.6
This version includes minor text changes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Version 1.2.5
## Enhances
**Using built-in pdf library**
The program now uses a built in library for handling the pdf files, which makes it work in offline mode aswell.

**Removed setting to toggle ribbon on and off**
This has been made to a setting inside the obsidian program itself, hence it's not needed in the plugin settings anymore.

**Renamed settings**
Made some minor changes the text in the settings.

**Improved Command Reliability**
Commands now only run when conditions are met, improving reliability and preventing unnecessary actions.

**New attachments folder path structure**
Previous the attachments folder should be specified in the settings. Now it uses the default attachments folder from the obsidian settings.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Version 1.2.4
## Bug fix
**Broken image link**
Fixed a major bug where folder and files with white space would not create a working image reference in the note.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Version 1.2.3
## Features
**Header Size Selection** 
You can now choose the size of the header to be inserted above each image. Options include h1, h2, h3, h4, and h5, giving you more control over the formatting of your notes.

**Header Extraction Sensitivity Adjustment** 
Added a new setting to fine-tune the sensitivity of the header extraction algorithm. This allows you to increase detection sensitivity for missing headers or reduce it to prevent false positives. The default sensitivity is set to 1.2.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Version 1.2.2

## New features
### Insert headers (BETA)
It is now possible to toggle the option to generate headers for each image based on pdf page analysis.
The setting is available in the plugin settings.
This feature is in beta and might not work as expected with some pdf files. I am working on settings to control the how aggresive the header exctraction is.

releases.description: This small update changes the order on the settings.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Version 1.2.0
This update introduces several exciting new features to enhance your experience.

## New Features
### Batch Image Insertion
A new setting allows you to choose between two different methods for inserting images:
**Procedural:** Images are generated and inserted one at a time.
**Batch:** All images are generated first, then inserted simultaneously for a more streamlined process.

### Custom Image Quality
When converting PDFs, you can now adjust the image quality to suit your needs. The default setting is 1x, but you can reduce it to as low as 0.5x for smaller file sizes and improved performance, or increase it up to 2x for the highest image clarity.

### Optional Line Between Images
You can now toggle the option to include or exclude an empty line beneath each image. This setting is easily adjustable in the preferences.
